zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Clifton於1570年出生在英國薩默塞特巴林頓時，她的父親Sir John Clifton33歲，母親Anne Stanley Baroness Monteagle27歲。她於1582年與Sir Amyas Bampfylde MP在英國薩默塞特巴林頓結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1630年在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴林頓享年60歲，埋葬在英國戴文諾斯 莫爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Clifton于1570年出生在英国薩默塞特巴林頓时，她的父亲Sir John Clifton33岁，母亲Anne Stanley Baroness Monteagle27岁。她于1582年与Sir Amyas Bampfylde MP在英国薩默塞特巴林頓结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1630年在英国英格兰薩默塞特巴林頓享年60岁，埋葬在英国戴文諾斯 莫爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Clifton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the very numerous places called Clifton, from Old English clif ‘slope’ (see Cliff ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cliff
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cliff(e), Cle(e)ve, or Clive, from Old English clif ‘slope, bank, cliff’, or a topographic name from the same word. The Old English word was used not only in the sense of modern English cliff but also of much gentler slopes and frequently also of a riverbank or seashore.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Clifton was born in 1570, in Barrington, Somerset, England, her father, Sir John Clifton, was 33 and her mother, Anne Stanley Baroness Monteagle, was 27. She married Sir Amyas Bampfylde MP in 1582, in Barrington, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1630, in Barrington, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in North Molton, Devon, England.

Elle est décédée en 1630, à Barrington, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à North Molton, Devon, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cliftonが1570年にイギリスソメルセトバーリントンで生まれた時，父Sir John Cliftonは33歳，母Anne Stanley Baroness Monteagleは27歳であった。1582年にイギリスソメルセトバーリントンでSir Amyas Bampfylde MPと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1630年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトバーリントンで60歳で死去し，イギリスデボンノース モルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Clifton(이)가 1570년 영국 서머세트 배링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Clifton(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Stanley Baroness Monteagle(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1582년 영국 서머세트 배링턴에서 Sir Amyas Bampfylde MP(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1630년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 배링턴에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 노르스 모턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Clifton родилась в 1570 г. в местности баррингтон, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Clifton было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Stanley Baroness Monteagle, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Amyas Bampfylde MP в 1582 г. в местности баррингтон, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1630 г. в местности баррингтон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности норт молтон, девон, Англия.
